page: OVHcloud b2-30 is a general-purpose virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs, utilizing an Intel Core Processor (Haswell, no TSX) running at 2.0 GHz. The processor lacks hyperthreading and is equipped with a 16 MB L3 cache, 4 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache. It supports AVX, AES, BMI2, and FMA instruction sets, but lacks AVX-512 and AMX extensions. The instance is configured with 30.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.75 GB of RAM per core, and includes 200 GB of local SSD storage.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance profile. Single-core CPU performance is weak, falling into the bottom 25% for stress-ng, Geekbench, and single-threaded compression tests. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, as demonstrated by stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores. Memory performance exhibits a significant tradeoff: cached small-block read bandwidth is average at 664.72 GB/sec, but uncached large-block read bandwidth is weak at 34.85 GB/sec, and large-block write bandwidth is poor at 12.29 GB/sec, landing in the bottom 10%.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and balanced resource distribution, the b2-30 is suited for workloads that benefit from multi-threaded execution without requiring extreme single-core speed or high memory write bandwidth. It achieves average performance in static web serving at 7.46 GB/sec, Redis SET operations at 10.05 million operations per second, and database operations. It is also capable of handling software compilation and light LLM inference on small models, though medium models show weak performance. This makes it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for general-purpose applications, multi-threaded utility tasks, and standard database hosting.
description: OVHcloud b2-30 is a general-purpose virtual server featuring 8 dedicated Intel Haswell vCPUs and 8 physical cores without hyperthreading, clocked at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 30.0 GB of RAM and 200 GB of local SSD storage on a KVM hypervisor. While single-core CPU performance and uncached large-block memory write speeds are weak, the instance delivers average multi-core processing, static web serving throughput, and database execution speeds. This resource profile provides a balanced 3.75 GB of memory per core, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, Redis caching, and standard database hosting. It supports AVX, AES, and FMA instruction sets, though it lacks AVX-512 capabilities. The instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.
og_description: OVHcloud b2-30 is a general-purpose virtual server with 8 dedicated Intel Haswell vCPUs, 30 GB RAM, and 200 GB local SSD storage, delivering balanced multi-core performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: OVHcloud b2-30 is a general-purpose KVM server with 8 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 30 GB RAM, and 200 GB SSD, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A dedicated eight-core virtual server with balanced memory and local SSD storage for multi-threaded workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel Haswell vCPUs and 8 physical cores without hyperthreading
30.0 GB of system memory and 200 GB of local SSD storage
Average multi-core CPU and database benchmark performance
Weak single-core processing and uncached memory write bandwidth
Well-suited for static web serving, Redis, and software compilation
